notes: Bucket routing derived from Ryan's reply 2026-04-21. skip_inbox=true removes INBOX label (stays accessible via sidebar). Priority buckets kept in inbox. Classifier decides category; this table says what label + whether to skip inbox.

project.description: Emails tied to a specific active project. Uses project_registry.json for label name. Auto-creates new label if classifier returns project_hint not in registry and confidence >= 0.7.

team_daily.label: 2. Team/Daily Accomplishments PH
team_daily.description: Kharene + Kim daily accomplishment threads. In-inbox per Q5.

bid_invite.label: 3. Bids/Invites
bid_invite.description: GC bid invites + Joseph's FWD bid invites. Out of main inbox per Q2.

vendor.label: 4. Vendors/Pricing
vendor.description: Supplier pricing for bid prep. Out of main inbox. If project_hint present, ALSO apply project label.

admin_ops.description: Banking, insurance, licensing. Stays in inbox.

client_inbound.description: Brand-new customer inquiries. Stays in inbox for Ryan visibility.

other.label: 5. Archive/Review
other.description: Classifier confidence low or doesn't fit above. Stays in inbox with faint label so Allen can spot drift.

priority_overrides.description: Overlays on top of bucket routing. Apply additional IMPORTANT flag to ring Ryan's phone. Per Q9 he wants ring on everything EXCEPT bid_invite + vendor/pricing requests from PH estimators.

rules.description: Default-mark as IMPORTANT unless in the 2 quiet buckets.

sender_hints.description: Override classifier when sender is an obvious pattern. Applied AFTER classification as a sanity check.

rules.note: Autodesk Construction Cloud pings — classifier must extract project name from subject/body.

gc_priority_senders.description: Ryan flagged these GCs (Q2 dodge = treat all equal). Keep list for future priority tuning. Not used in v1 routing.
